source_pr.title: lightning: precheck cluster region and available space
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

A supplement to https://github.com/pingcap/br/pull/1387.

If there are too many empty regions in cluster or regions distribution is unbalanced, lightning's import speed will become very slow. This PR adds more checks about empty regions and regions distribution before lightning restore.

### What is changed and how it works?

Fetch regions information from PD and check the empty regions for each TiKV store and make sure the count is less than a safe value. For regions whether the distribution is balanced, this PR calculate the ratio of the regions count of the store with most regions to store with least regions and make sure it is less than a safe value.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [x] Integration test

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

```release-note
lightning: precheck cluster region and available space
```

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it assumed a deterministic ordering of a task slice (e.g., asserting `tasks[j].taskID == j`) even though the returned order was not guaranteed. This PR stabilizes the assertion by sorting the tasks by `taskID` before checking their sequence.
fix_pattern: Sort slices before order-sensitive assertions (e.g., sort tasks by taskID before checking sequence).

evidence: evidence: sort.Slice(tasks, func(i, j int) bool { (br/pkg/lightning/restore/meta_manager_test.go)

files: br/pkg/lightning/restore/check_info.go, br/pkg/lightning/restore/check_template.go, br/pkg/lightning/restore/meta_manager.go, br/pkg/lightning/restore/meta_manager_test.go, br/pkg/lightning/restore/restore.go, br/pkg/lightning/restore/restore_test.go, br/pkg/lightning/restore/table_restore.go
